核心价值观.描述: 李时珍以医为业，以救治百姓疾苦为终身使命。他行医济世，发现前人本草著作有诸多错误，决心编纂一部准确的药典以利民生。
原文引用: There were famous herbals from ancient times, but all these, to the number of about 1,000, were embodied by Li Shijen in the compilation of Bencao gangmu in the 16th century ce. ——Britannica医学史
A valuable volume on herbs and medicine that was the fruit of 26 years of labour. ——Britannica教育史

核心价值观.描述: 李时珍在编纂《本草纲目》时，以严谨的态度考证每一种药物。他不盲从前人，而是通过实地调研、亲自采集标本来确认药性，体现了求实辨误的科学精神。
原文引用: Completed in 1578, the book was in part a compilation of other smaller works of the same kind. It contained descriptions of 1,094 herbs and 444 animal and 275 mineral substances. ——Britannica百科
The use of drugs is mainly to restore the harmony of the yin and the yang. ——Britannica医学史

核心价值观.描述: 《本草纲目》既是对古代本草学传统的系统整理，又在编纂方法和内容上有所创新。李时珍综合前人成果，耗时26年完成这部集大成之作。
原文引用: It contained descriptions of 1,094 herbs and 444 animal and 275 mineral substances. Li described such seemingly modern processes as distillation and the uses of mercury, ephedrine, chaulmoogra oil, iodine, and even smallpox inoculation. ——Britannica百科

Produced great illustrated encyclopedias, including Bencao gangmu (late 16th century; 'Index of Native Herbs'), a monumental materia medica listing 1,892 herbal concoctions and their applications. ——Britannica中国文学条目

政治立场.描述: 李时珍为科举未能中式之儒士，后弃儒从医，专心医学研究。其政治立场资料有限，但从其以医济世的选择来看，他倾向于以医术服务社会而非仕途。
原文引用: Li Shizhen (born 1518, China—died 1593) was a Chinese scholar of the Ming dynasty. ——Britannica百科

底线清单.描述: 李时珍编纂《本草纲目》时，最核心的底线是确保药物记载的准确性。他花费26年时间，遍访各地，亲自考证，以纠正前人本草中的错误。
原文引用: A valuable volume on herbs and medicine that was the fruit of 26 years of labour. This work, in 52 volumes, has been frequently revised and reprinted and is still authoritative. ——Britannica医学史

自我定位.描述: 李时珍自视为医学传统的继承者和本草学的集大成者。他以学者（scholar）自居，以严谨的学术态度完成了中国历史上最具影响力的本草学巨著。
原文引用: Li Shizhen was a Chinese scholar of the Ming dynasty (1368–1644) who compiled a highly influential materia medica, the Bencao gangmu. ——Britannica百科
This work, in 52 volumes, has been frequently revised and reprinted and is still authoritative. ——Britannica医学史
